nextSession.thesis: AFTER-HOURS (7/13 cash session CLOSED) — position OVERNIGHT into the marquee catalyst of the month: June CPI + a HEAVY Q2 big-bank open (JPM/WFC/BAC/C/GS) BOTH land pre-open TOMORROW (Tue 7/14 8:30 ET / ~7am ET). The completed 7/13 bar closed de-risked (SPY 749.17 −0.54% on the day, QQQ 711.74 CLOSED under its 50-day, MSFT still bear-stacked) — a mild pre-print de-risk, NOT a break; all core still bull-stacked. VIX closed 17.16 (low/calm, below the 3-pt regime threshold, positive-gamma / orderly). Posture for the 7/14 session: HOLD the structural bull-stacks, HEDGE the overnight CPI gap while VIX (17.16) is still cheap, take the tool-aligned XLI→XLE rotation only on an RS-cross, and stay OUT of havens (GLD/SLV/BTC all Downtrend). Calm is a positioning window into a binary, not an all-clear.

actions.subject: Overnight SPY/SPX downside into 7/14 CPI + bank open
actions.rationale: CPI and a five-name big-bank open BOTH hit pre-open tomorrow into a short-vol tape that CLOSED de-risked (SPY −0.54% on the day, QQQ lost its 50-day). VIX 17.16 keeps defined-risk downside cheap; the overnight-into-CPI gap is unhedged in the price. Buy the protection in the overnight/pre-market window BEFORE the 8:30 number, not after.
actions.structuralAnchor: SPY 749.17 bull-stack (20>50>200), only −1.12% off the 52-week high (~757.7) with VIX 17.16 — spot near the highs + cheap vol = maximum asymmetry to hedge a known binary.
actions.trigger: Put protection / put-spread or a small VIX-call overlay in the overnight/pre-market before the 8:30 CPI; size to the CPI gap, not a trend reversal.
actions.invalidation: A cool/in-line CPI + orderly bank open with SPY reclaiming ~753 and VIX back under 16 — let the hedge decay; do not roll it into a standing short.

actions.subject: SPY / QQQ / SPMO / VGT core longs
actions.rationale: The core is still bull-stacked with strong medium-term momentum (SPY mom126/252 +9.23%/+21.04%; QQQ mom63 +16.6%, mom252 +28.75%). The 7/13 fade is a pullback to support ahead of a binary, not a structural break — QQQ closing −0.54% under its 50-day (~715.6) is a caution flag to WATCH, not a sell.
actions.trigger: Hold while SPY holds the rising 50-day (~741); let the core carry, do not add into the CPI print.
actions.invalidation: A confirmed daily close below the SPY 50-day (~741) or a broad loss of the QQQ/VGT/SPMO bull-stacks.

actions.subject: Trim rolling XLI / cooling XLK → add XLE
actions.rationale: The tool-aligned 2-week RRG accel says energy is the freshest rotation INTO: XLE Improving ↑ (accel +1.05, the board’s best) with rsMom6m +14.34 and +11.25% over its 200-day, while XLI is Weakening ↓ (accel −2.40, the sharpest roll on the board) and XLK is Weakening ↓/out. Softer crude fading the CPI headline tail keeps this an early, defensible cyclical rotation.
actions.structuralAnchor: XLE Improving quadrant, accel +1.05 (best on the board), tangled MA stack turning; XLI Weakening ↓, accel −2.40 (rolling over).
actions.trigger: Add XLE on its RS-ratio crossing 100 (into the Leading quadrant); fund it by trimming XLI on any failure of its rising 50-day.
actions.invalidation: XLE acceleration rolls back negative or oil (USO score 29.6, Mixed) breaks lower, aborting the energy turn.

actions.subject: Extended semis (SOXX) into weakening RRG + CPI vol
actions.rationale: SOXX is the strongest structure on the board (+43.76% over its 200-day) but that is exactly the problem into a binary: Weakening ↓/out on the 2-week accel and stretched far above trend. Trim the extension ahead of CPI; keep the core — this is de-risking a stretched leader, not selling the theme.
actions.structuralAnchor: SOXX +43.76% over its 200-day, Weakening ↓ (rsMom below 100); semis broadly red on the 5-day flag near-term profit-taking under the surface.
actions.trigger: Trim into strength / on SOXX losing its rising 20-day; re-add only when rsMom crosses back above 100.
actions.invalidation: SOXX reclaims a rising RS-momentum (>100) with breadth improving — the leadership re-accelerates and the trim was early.

actions.subject: Stay OUT of GLD / SLV / BTC-USD·IBIT (no haven bid)
actions.rationale: Even into a CPI binary there is NO defensive bid in real assets: real-assets-lab scores GLD 14.9 (Downtrend), SLV 0 (washed out, worst on the board), BTC-USD 39.5 / IBIT 32.2 (both Downtrend). Agriculture (DBA 54.7) is the only firm real-asset trend. Do not reach for havens as the CPI hedge — the hedge is cheap index vol, not gold.
actions.structuralAnchor: GLD mom63 −15.96, SLV mom63 −24.49 (washed out), BTC-USD score 39.5 — a risk-on / disinflationary-at-the-margin real-asset tape, not a fear tape.
actions.trigger: Re-engage metals only if GLD reclaims an uptrend AND the SLV model score lifts off 0; re-engage BTC only above its downtrend.
actions.invalidation: A hot CPI sparks a genuine safe-haven gold breakout (GLD reclaims trend) — then the no-haven read is stale and gold becomes the tell.

dataAsOf.macro: VIX 17.16 (Yahoo ^VIX live this run; options-chain CBOE VIX spot 17.1 confirms) — LOW / calm, BELOW the 3-pt regime-change threshold, so the regime is positive-gamma / orderly into the print. NOTE: the deterministic composite score reads 0 because Fear&Greed was NOT fetched this run (F&G null → VIX-only composite), NOT because VIX is missing — VIX is present and low. VIX term structure (VIX9D/VIX3M) NOT in the Tier-A slice — re-pull live. WTI CARRIED/STALE ~$71.5 (last web-verify; USO score 29.6, Mixed) — softer crude is a softer-headline-CPI tail if it holds into 8:30 tomorrow.
dataAsOf.events: Near-term calendar CARRIED from the config macroEvents (7/11 web verification vs BLS / FRED / Census / U.S. Treasury / CME FedWatch / Kalshi); dates are structural/confirmed, consensus print figures remain ESTIMATES — RE-VERIFY each at release. IMMINENT (T−1): June CPI Tue 7/14 8:30am ET (BLS) COLLIDES with a HEAVY Q2 big-bank open — JPMorgan + Wells Fargo + Bank of America + Citigroup + Goldman Sachs ALL pre-open (~7am ET) the SAME morning (Morgan Stanley 7/15). Then June PPI 7/15 8:30am + MS; June retail sales + weekly jobless claims 7/16 8:30am; monthly OPEX 7/17. FOMC 7/29 2:00pm (non-SEP, no new dots) collides with the MSFT FY26 Q4 print AMC 7/29; July NFP 8/7. FOMC path (CARRIED, CME FedWatch / Kalshi 7/11): ~79% HOLD / ~2% cut / ~15-18% HIKE tail — funds 3.50-3.75% held four straight meetings, no 2026 cuts, hawkish; a hot 7/14 CPI re-arms the hike tail rather than merely delaying cuts.

fearGreed.band: not fetched this run (F&G null → composite is VIX-only; do not assume a band)

vix.term: VIX 17.16 (Yahoo ^VIX live this run; CBOE options-chain spot 17.1 confirms) — LOW / calm, well UNDER the 3-pt regime-change threshold, so this is an orderly positive-gamma tape, NOT a stress regime. Term structure (VIX9D/VIX3M) NOT in the Tier-A slice — re-pull live. Falsifier: a VIX push >19-20 or IVTS crossing into backwardation (>1.0).

breadth.note: STRUCTURALLY BROAD but leadership breadth is COOLING with NO clean Leading sector this bar. Tool-aligned RRG (2-week RS accel, completed 7/13 bar): rotation INTO XLE (Improving ↑, accel +1.05, best on the board) and XLC (Improving ↑, accel +0.90, but bear-stacked below its 200-day); rotation OUT / Weakening ↓ for XLK (accel −0.14), XLV (−1.51), XLI (−2.40, sharpest roll), and XLF now Peaking ⚠ (accel −0.17). The structural RS leader remains XLK (+18.77% over its 200-day) but it is cooling on the accel. Strongest positive acceleration on the board is the MAGS complex (accel +1.48). Lagging/neutral: XLY, XLP, XLB, XLRE, XLU. Proxy from sector-member bars, not an exchange breadth feed. Author rotation from RRG state/accel, NOT the raw 1-month RS level.

regime.note: STRUCTURE FIRST, completed 7/13 close: a healthy but EXTENDED tech-led bull near all-time highs, closing de-risked ahead of a binary. SPY 749.17 bull-stacked (20>50>200), −1.12% off its 52-week high, +8.33% over a rising 200-day, mom126/252 +9.23%/+21.04%. 2nd distinct bar after Fri 7/10's close (moderate persistence); the day's de-risk (SPY −0.54%, QQQ CLOSED under its 50-day) is pre-catalyst positioning, not a regime flip — VIX 17.16 is low/calm, under the 3-pt threshold. Three features on top of the intact bull: (1) a LEADERSHIP shift with NO clean Leading sector — XLK/SOXX Weakening ↓/out despite the board’s best structural RS, XLF Peaking ⚠ right before the bank open, rotation INTO is early (XLE Improving +1.05; XLC Improving/bear-stacked), MAGS the strongest positive accel (+1.48); (2) the lone STRUCTURAL crack, MSFT (bear-stack, −27.41% off its high, mom5 +1.10) not reclaiming its 50-day (~403); (3) NO haven bid — GLD 14.9 / SLV 0 / BTC 39.5 all Downtrend, only agriculture (DBA 54.7) firm. All under a HAWKISH-HOLD Fed (7/29 ~79% hold / ~15-18% HIKE, no 2026 cuts). Posture for 7/14: HOLD the bull-stacks; HEDGE the overnight CPI gap while VIX (17.16) is cheap; take XLI→XLE only on an RS-cross; TRIM stretched semis; stay OUT of havens; do NOT call an MSFT bottom until it reclaims ~403.

backdrop.primaryTrend: Extended structural bull near all-time highs, de-risked into the 7/14 CPI + bank-open binary. SPY 749.17 bull-stacked (20>50>200), −1.12% off the 52-week high, +8.33% over a rising 200-day; SPMO/VGT bull-stacked, QQQ bull-stacked but CLOSED under its 50-day on the 7/13 bar. Leadership rotates UNDER an intact index: tech/semis cooling on the 2-week accel, no clean Leading sector, XLF peaking into its own earnings, MAGS turning up on thin breadth. The single structural exception is MSFT (bear-stack, −27.41% off high) unrepaired.
backdrop.trendEvidence: SPY mom126 +9.23 / mom252 +21.04, +1.14% over the 50-day (~740.7), +8.33% over the 200-day (~691.6). QQQ mom63 +16.6, mom252 +28.75, but −0.54% UNDER its 50-day (~715.6) on the completed 7/13 close. SPMO +18.16% / VGT +16.52% over their 200-days. Sector breadth broad — 8/11 bull-stacked and above their 200-days. SOXX 12/12 above the 200-day, +43.76% over it (extended). Trend-intact readings; the close-of-day pullback is shallow and holds structural support.

support: 50-day ~715.6 (spot −0.54% — CLOSED below it on 7/13, the hold-or-fail line)

support: in a DOWNTREND — bear-stack; mom5 +1.1 firming but no base confirmed

backdrop.macroCycle: HAWKISH-HOLD Fed: funds 3.50-3.75% held four straight meetings, no 2026 cuts priced, hawkish. 7/29 FOMC (non-SEP) modal HOLD (~79%) with a LIVE ~15-18% HIKE tail / ~2% cut (CARRIED, CME FedWatch 7/11; one 7/11 read put the hike tail as high as ~38%). A hot 7/14 CPI re-arms the hike tail rather than merely delaying cuts — the asymmetry the short-vol tape is underpricing. WTI carried/STALE ~$71.5; USO Mixed with mom21 −12.3 — softer crude is a softer-headline-CPI tail if it holds. Re-verify the priced path + WTI live at the CPI release.
globalBackdrop: GLOBAL ROTATION is Asia-led and confirms the onshore semis / AI-hardware supply chain: EWS (Singapore) leads at 73.3/100 vs ACWI (rel21 +9.05, Strong uptrend), EWT (Taiwan) 73.2 next (rel63 +24.2, rel126 +46.51), then EWC 58.7, EWJ 56, EWY 55.8, EWU 54.1 — but EVERY leader carries an FX headwind, so the equity strength is local-currency, not a weak-dollar tailwind (deep-link: global-rotation-lab.html).

AI-CAPEX CYCLE is the dominant secular driver — MSFT's structural breakdown (bear-stack, −27.41% off its high) vs the AI-hardware/semis bull-stacks (VGT/SPMO near highs, SOXX +43.76% over its 200-day, AMD mom63 +118.1, MU mom63 +122.8) IS the "show-me" (software) vs "build-it" (hardware/semis) dispersion at the single-name level. The MSFT capex guide 7/29 is the tell (deep-link: ai-capex-strategy-lab.html).

REAL ASSETS show NO haven bid even on the eve of the CPI binary: GLD Downtrend (14.9, mom63 −16.01), SLV washed out (0, mom63 −24.49, vol 76.6), BTC/IBIT Downtrend (39.5 / 32.2). Agriculture (DBA 54.7) is the only firm real-asset trend; broad commodities (DBC 42.7 / PDBC 42.9) and oil (USO 29.6) are Mixed. Net: risk-on, disinflationary-at-the-margin (deep-link: real-assets-lab.html).
backdrop.pricedIn: A benign-to-in-line CPI and an orderly bank open are the base case the short-vol tape (VIX 17.16, low) is positioned for — the ~47% "in-line" scenario. What is NOT priced: the overnight-into-CPI gap, a hot print that re-arms the ~15-18% hike tail, and any bank-earnings read-through that confirms/denies the XLF Peaking flip.
backdrop.asymmetry: Skewed to the downside into 7/14: spot is −1.12% off the high with VIX 17.16 (low) and QQQ closing under its 50-day, so a hot CPI or a bad bank open has more room to move price than an in-line print (already largely discounted). Upside case (cool CPI) lets the cooling leaders re-accelerate and fades the hike tail.
backdrop.whatWouldChangeIt: A confirmed SPY close below the 50-day (~741) or QQQ failing to reclaim ~716; VIX (17.16) pushing >19-20 or the term structure inverting; XLF's Peaking flip confirmed by a rejected bank-earnings open; or MSFT reclaiming its 50-day (~403) to end the lone structural downtrend. A hot CPI is the single fastest regime-changer given the hawkish-hold hike tail.

attention.why: A Peaking flip that a rejected earnings open would CONFIRM is a high-information event: it tells you whether the last cyclical leader is topping. Watch the bank reaction as the sector-rotation tell, not just the single names.

events.psychologyNote: The next month’s labor anchor — outside the near-term window but the follow-through to whether July prints a hawkish or a soft-landing regime.

psychology.read: NAMED REGIME: a calm, positive-gamma, tech-led bull with COOLING leadership and NO clean Leading sector, closing de-risked ON THE EVE of a binary. The crowd’s posture is constructive-but-crowded — long the intact bull-stacks and positioned for an in-line CPI (~47%) — with VIX 17.16 (low/calm, live) confirming the short-vol, orderly tape. NOTE the deterministic composite score of 0 is VIX-only (Fear&Greed was NOT fetched this run), NOT a measured neutral and NOT a VIX data gap — VIX is present and low. The completed 7/13 bar closed with the pre-catalyst tell in the tape: QQQ 711.74 CLOSED under its 50-day, XLF sits Peaking ⚠ right before the bank open, and no sector is cleanly Leading. This is the classic pre-catalyst setup — the tape looks orderly because dealer gamma is positive and no one has been forced to hedge, NOT because the risk is small. What would FALSIFY the read: a VIX push >19-20 or term-structure inversion (regime flip), a confirmed SPY close below the 50-day (~741) or QQQ failing to reclaim ~716 (structural break), a hot CPI re-arming the hike tail (the biggest single falsifier), or MAGS broadening + tech re-accelerating (bull re-leads and the cooling was just a pause).
inputs: SPY 749.17 bull-stack (20>50>200), +1.14% over the 50-day (~740.7), +8.33% over the 200-day (~691.6), −1.12% off the 52-week high (~757.7); mom126/252 +9.23% / +21.04% (Tier-A completed 7/13 close)

2nd DISTINCT daily bar of the theme (after Fri 7/10’s close); the 7/13 morning/pre-close runs were the same forming bar, so this is moderate — not high — persistence. QQQ 711.74 CLOSED −0.54% UNDER its 50-day on this bar
RRG rotation: NO clean Leading sector; XLF Peaking ⚠; XLK/SOXX Weakening/out; XLE Improving/into (early, accel +1.05); MAGS strongest positive accel (+1.48)

VIX 17.16 (low/calm, Yahoo ^VIX live; CBOE spot 17.1 confirms); Fear&Greed NOT fetched → composite is VIX-only (score 0 = VIX in the neutral zone, NOT a data gap). Term structure not in the Tier-A slice (re-pull live)
Real assets: no haven bid even on the eve of the binary — GLD 14.9 / SLV 0 / BTC 39.5 all Downtrend; DBA 54.7 the only firm trend (risk-on, disinflationary-at-the-margin)
Catalyst cluster, T−0 overnight: 7/14 CPI + heavy bank open, 7/15 PPI+MS, 7/16 retail+claims, 7/17 OPEX, 7/29 FOMC+MSFT — under a hawkish-hold Fed (~79% hold / ~15-18% hike tail, carried)
psychology.tiltOnProbabilities: The base case remains bullish-to-neutral (in-line CPI ~47%, bull holds), but the completed-bar evidence — QQQ closing under its 50-day, XLF Peaking into its own earnings, no clean Leading sector — tilts probability weight toward the DOWNSIDE tail into 7/14, and the missing VIX means the market’s own fear gauge is UNMEASURED into the print. Net: same structural thesis, higher hedge value into the CPI (establish it in the overnight/pre-market window since the cash session is closed), rotation calls (XLI→XLE) taken only on an RS-cross, and semis trimmed for the extension — not sold. Re-fetch VIX + the term structure at the open before sizing anything off the vol read.

groups.label: Magnificent 7

notable.reason: group leader at its 52-week high (pctFrom52wHigh 0), only bull-stacked member
biggest 21d/5d surge on the board, but still bear-stacked (−16.6% off its high)
the lone structural crack (−27.41% off high); mom5 +1.1 firming, not a reclaim of the 50-day
weakest 5-day (−5.96); bear-stacked laggard to confirm the complex turn

groups.note: MAGS is Improving ↑/into with the board’s strongest positive acceleration (+1.48) — the mega-cap complex turning UP on the 2-week accel even though only 1/7 members are bull-stacked (breadth 1/7). Leadership is narrow and top-heavy: AAPL at its 52-week high and META’s +15.12 mom21 / +9.4 mom5 surge carry the complex, NVDA is bouncing (+4.08 mom5, tangled), while MSFT (the crack, −27.41% off high) and TSLA (−5.96 mom5) lag. An EARLY-accumulation signal under a weak-breadth surface — WATCH for broadening (>1/7 bull-stacked) to confirm, don’t chase into the CPI binary. Author from the RRG state/accel, not the raw 1-month RS.

groups.label: Semiconductors

notable.reason: strongest trend (mom63 +118.08, +84.57% over its 200-day), still bull-stacked but −3.2 on the 5-day
sharpest reversal (mom21 −13.86, mom5 −12.71) — profit-taking under the surface
most extended (mom63 +122.82, +100.15% over its 200-day) and red on the 5-day
weakest 5-day (−6.69) among the bull-stacked leaders — near-term profit-taking

groups.note: SOXX is Weakening ↓/out on the 2-week accel (rsMom1m −1.26) but keeps the board’s strongest structure — 8/12 bull-stacked, 12/12 above the 200-day, +43.76% over it. The extension is the risk into a binary: AMD/TXN/AMAT/LRCX/KLAC/MU are all far above trend while MRVL (mom21 −13.86 / mom5 −12.71) and TSM/LRCX/KLAC/MU red on the 5-day show near-term profit-taking. TRIM the extension into strength / on a lost 20-day; re-add when rsMom crosses back above 100 — de-risking a stretched leader, not selling the AI-hardware theme. Author from the RRG state/accel, not the raw 1-month RS.

QQQ.status: $711.74 — BULL-STACK (20>50>200) but CLOSED −0.54% UNDER its 50-day (~715.6) on the completed 7/13 bar — the structural hold-or-fail line, and this is now a CONFIRMED daily close below (not just an intraday slip), the caution the prior runs were watching for. Still +11.70% over the 200-day (~637.2), −4.51% off its 52-week high (~745.4); mom63 +16.60, mom252 +28.75, mom5 −1.53 (soft into the close). Tech is COOLING on the 2-week accel (XLK Weakening ↓/out) — decelerating momentum plus a lost 50-day, not yet a bull-stack break — HOLD, watch for a 50-day reclaim vs a second close below, carry the defined-risk hedge into CPI.

SPMO.status: $149.73 — BULL-STACK, +0.51% over the 50-day, +18.16% over the 200-day; mom63 +21.58, mom252 +34.32, mom5 −2.12 (pulled back with tech into the close). The strongest structural trend in the watchlist and a top-3 risk-adjusted momentum ETF (behind JMOM on the 6M balanced ranking). Core long; hold, add only on strength, don’t chase into CPI.

VGT.status: $115.58 — BULL-STACK, +0.18% over the 50-day, +16.52% over the 200-day; mom63 +24.55, mom252 +37.67, mom5 −0.68. A top-3 risk-adjusted momentum ETF. Pure-tech core long; hold through the cooling, the bull-stack + RS are intact even with the QQQ 50-day loss.

MSFT.status: $390.99 — BEAR-STACK, −2.90% below its 50-day (~402.7), −11.17% below its 200-day (~440.1), −27.41% off its 52-week high (~538.7); mom5 +1.10 (firming intraday), mom21 −1.60, mom63 +5.65. A mild stabilization inside a downtrend — NOT a trend change until it reclaims the 50-day (~403). The lone structural crack while QQQ (now sub-50-day itself) and VGT/SPMO stay bull-stacked; the 7/29 capex guide is the tell for the AI "show-me vs build-it" dispersion.

experimental.title: Pre-catalyst de-risk detector (intraday-fade + VIX-uptick + leader-flip)
experimental.note: Method: on the session BEFORE a Tier-1 macro binary (CPI/PPI/FOMC/NFP), score a composite "de-risk tell" = sign(index close drift) + sign(VIX day-change) + count of sectors rolling out (Leading/Improving→Weakening/Peaking) on the fresh bar (this run, completed 7/13 into 7/14 CPI: QQQ closed −0.54% and lost its 50-day, mom5 −1.53; VIX 17.16 low/calm but the Tier-A slice carries no prior-day VIX, so the day-change SIGN leg needs a second stored read — level is present, delta is not; XLI Weakening ↓ accel −2.40 rolling out = risk-off tilt). Then event-study the forward CPI-day gap and 1-/3-day return conditioned on the composite, to test whether a pre-print de-risk predicts a smaller adverse gap (crowd already hedged) or a larger one (crowd still offside). Inputs: open→close index drift, VIX day-change (persist prior close so the sign leg is computable), per-sector RRG state transitions, the dated catalyst. No owning tool captures the pre-catalyst de-risk state-transition timing today.

experimental.title: Rotation-flip detector (Leading→Peaking)
experimental.note: Method: flag any sector whose RRG state crosses out of a strong quad (Leading→Peaking, or Improving→Weakening) with negative acceleration on a fresh bar (this run, completed 7/13: XLI Weakening ↓ accel −2.40 rolling out of a bull-stack while XLE Improves ↑ accel +1.05 rotates in — the XLI→XLE hand-off), then event-study the forward 5/10-day sector return conditioned on whether the coincident earnings/catalyst confirmed or denied the roll (here: the 7/14 money-center bank open is the financials/industrials-cycle tell for whether the XLI roll extends). Inputs: per-sector rsRatio/rsMom/accel history + the sector’s next scheduled catalyst. No owning tool captures the state-transition timing today.
